title: Podman container fails to establish outbound network connectivity if host boots without WAN and gets connected later
body:
### Issue Description

We're observing an issue where containers managed by Podman are unable to access the network if the host system boots with no WAN connectivity and gets connected via Ethernet later.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Disconnect all WAN interfaces.
2. Reboot the host system.
3. After boot is complete and containers have started (or start them manually).
4. Verify no connection is available in Host and in container.
```
# host
curl https://8.8.8.8

# container
podman run -it oraclelinux bash
curl https://8.8.8.8  
```
5. Connect Ethernet (eth0).
6. Wait for the eth0 to be connected and then confirm that the host has connectivity (e.g., curl https://google.com works on host).
7. Update container DNS using `podman network update <NETWORK> --dns-add <DNS>`.
8. Check connectivity on host, we are seeing the below behaviour on host.
```
curl https://8.8.8.8       # --> Returns 302 Moved
curl https://google.com    # --> Returns 301 Moved
```
9. Try the same from inside podman container — container has no connectivity.
```
podman run -it oraclelinux bash
# Inside container:
curl https://8.8.8.8       # --> Fails with "Network unreachable"
curl https://google.com    # --> Fails with DNS timeout
```

Additionally:

- I can see the DNS is updated, can see value in `podman inspect` command
- correct values are present on the `etc/resolv.conf` on host.

### Describe the results you received

I can observe the connectivity is available on the HOST but the not able to connect from inside the container.
1. Check connectivity on host, we are seeing the below behaviour on host.
```
curl https://8.8.8.8       # --> Returns 302 Moved
curl https://google.com    # --> Returns 301 Moved
```
2. Try the same from inside podman container — container has no connectivity.
```
podman run -it oraclelinux bash
# Inside container:
curl https://8.8.8.8       # --> Fails with "Network unreachable"
curl https://google.com    # --> Fails with DNS timeout
```

### Describe the results you expected

Once the host has working WAN (e.g., via eth0), containers should also have outbound connectivity — both to raw IPs and via DNS.

### podman info output

comments.body: Are you running as root or rootless with, with what network settings are you actually starting the containers?

We have rootless container the same I have mentioned in the description to.
Below is the network settings I am using
```yaml
Networks:
  <my-network-name>:
    aliases:
      - <application-name>
netns:
  nsmode: bridge
```

What is the output of `podman unshare --rootless-netns sh -c "ip addr; ip route; ip -6 route"` then.

With pasta it is common that if your container starts before the network is ready/configured that it might pick the wrong interface/routes. But if no interfaces are available it should default to a link local address with a route.

In any case please try the latest versions if podman and pasta as upstream only support the latest versions.

```
$ podman unshare --rootless-netns sh -c "ip addr; ip route; ip -6 route"

       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

> 2: eth0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65520 qdisc fq_codel state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether d6:b4:34:51:94:f7 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 192.168.88.203/24 brd 192.168.88.255 scope global noprefixroute eth0

So you host is not really disconnected by the time the container starts? This clearly shows an eth0 interface with a valid ip address. It seems you have no default route for it though and because pasta copies the information from the host it keeps the routes and if there are none you container will have no default route either.

The pasta "local mode" only gets used if there is no interface on the host with an ip address (except localhost), see the pasta(1) man page. So I think this works as expected.

@sbrivio-rh When configuring a static address for pasta would it always add a default route? i.e. with such a containers.conf config:
```
[network]
pasta_options = ["-a", "10.0.2.0", "-n", "24", "-g", "10.0.2.2", "--dns-forward", "10.0.2.3"]
```

> [@sbrivio-rh](https://github.com/sbrivio-rh) When configuring a static address for pasta would it always add a default route?

Not always, that is, if the user configures a particular address, we'll still try to copy routes. However:

> i.e. with such a containers.conf config:
> 
> ```
> [network]
> pasta_options = ["-a", "10.0.2.0", "-n", "24", "-g", "10.0.2.2", "--dns-forward", "10.0.2.3"]
> ```

...in this example, `-g` configures the default gateway, hence the default route.

> In any case please try the latest versions if podman and pasta as upstream only support the latest versions.

@gaurangomar are you using the latest version of Podman and pasta for this example?

Originally I added the info after connecting the WAN connections(s), It looks like you are looking for the info before connecting WAN links.
Here is what I have. Apologies for the confusion. 
```
$ podman unshare --rootless-netns sh -c "ip addr; ip route; ip -6 route"
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host proto kernel_lo 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: tap0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65520 qdisc fq_codel state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 1a:80:d6:37:5a:7f brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 169.254.2.1/16 scope global tap0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::1880:d6ff:fe37:5a7f/64 scope link nodad tentative proto kernel_ll 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
default via 169.254.2.2 dev tap0 
169.254.0.0/16 dev tap0 proto kernel scope link src 169.254.2.1 
fe80::/64 dev tap0 proto kernel metric 256 pref medium
default via fe80::1 dev tap0 metric 1024 pref medium
```
@gaurangomar with which version of passt was this taken?

@sbrivio-rh 
```
# passt --version
passt 0^20250217.ga1e48a0-9.el9_6.x86_64

Please use the latest upstream version instead, as @Luap99 already asked in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26678#issuecomment-3109512183.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Hi Team, Wanted to confirm if only passt version update is required or should we update to new podman version (5.5/+) to overcome this issue?

If by "confirm" you mean "ask": I don't know, maybe it works with an older Podman as well, but here we only support the latest upstream versions. Ask your distribution / vendor for support with downstream versions.

I just ran into this issue on debian sid: `passt 0.0~git20260120.386b5f5-1`, `podman version 5.8.2`

the issue seems to stem from the rootless netns not getting updated when host interfaces or routing changes

steps to reproduce:

1. bring your WAN/internet connection down
2. force stop pasta: `killall -i -r 'pasta.*'`
3. ensure a new netns is started: `podman unshare --rootless-netns ip route`
4. enable your WAN connection
5. now your podman userns is stuck without a default route: `podman unshare --rootless-netns ping 8.8.8.8` should throw `ping: connect: Network is unreachable`
6. force restarting the netns process will restore connectivity

an interesting thing is that this only seems to affect containers that are attached to a `bridge` network, ie `podman run alpine ping 8.8.8.8` is fine, but `podman run --network=bridge alpine ping 8.8.8.8` is affected

I can't stress enough how bad it was hunting this bug down, it's very bad default behaviour
